notable_pick_trades: Acquired 3rd-round pick (No. 87) from Eagles via Jaelan Phillips trade; Acquired 3rd-round pick (No. 90) from Chargers via Titans/Jets; Traded Minkah Fitzpatrick to Jets for 7th-round pick (No. 238); Traded own 6th-round pick (No. 192) to Panthers Traded WR Jaylen Waddle and 4th (#111) to Broncos for 1st (#30), 3rd (#94), 4th (#130).
roster_context: Full-scale rebuild under new GM Jon-Eric Sullivan and HC Jeff Hafley (both from Green Bay). Released Tua Tagovailoa (post-June 1, ~$42.9M dead money), Tyreek Hill (failed physical), Bradley Chubb, James Daniels, Liam Eichenberg, and Jason Sanders. Traded Minkah Fitzpatrick to Jets. Lost Kader Kohou to Chiefs in free agency. Signed Malik Willis as presumptive starting QB on 3-year/$67.5M deal. Cap-strapped team making mostly one-year, low-cost signings. Building through the draft with five top-100 picks.

needs.explanation: The secondary is decimated. Traded Jalen Ramsey in 2024, lost Kader Kohou to Chiefs in FA, Rasul Douglas is a UFA who left. Current CB room is Cam Smith, Ethan Bonner, A.J. Green III, Marco Wilson, Darrell Baker Jr., and Alex Austin — mostly unproven depth pieces and special teamers. No legitimate CB1 on the roster. Multiple beat reporters and analysts identify this as the team's biggest defensive need.
needs.depth: A.J. Green III, Marco Wilson, Darrell Baker Jr., Alex Austin, Lonnie Johnson Jr. — all depth/special teams players

needs.explanation: Guard play was historically bad in 2025. Jonah Savaiinaea earned a 14.1 PFF pass-blocking grade as a rookie, ranking dead last among all guards. James Daniels was released, Cole Strange and Larry Borom left in FA. Signed Jamaree Salyer but he projects more as depth/swing option. Need a legitimate starting guard to protect Malik Willis. Beat writers say team prefers to use a top-100 pick on a guard.
needs.current_starter: LG Jonah Savaiinaea (needs major development), RG Jamaree Salyer (placeholder)
needs.depth: Andrew Meyer, Braeden Daniels, Josh Priebe, Kion Smith — very thin

needs.explanation: Traded Jaylen Waddle to Denver for draft capital including 1st-round pick (#30). WR room is now Malik Washington, Jalen Tolbert, Tutu Atwell — no proven WR1. This is now the most urgent need on the roster alongside CB. Must address in Round 1-2 of the draft.
needs.current_starter: Malik Washington (WR1 by default), Jalen Tolbert (WR2), Tutu Atwell (WR3)
needs.depth: Extremely thin. No established playmaker. Waddle departure leaves massive void.

needs.explanation: Traded Minkah Fitzpatrick to Jets for only a 7th-round pick. Elijah Campbell signed with Giants. Current safety room is Dante Trader Jr. (2nd-year, limited), Zayne Anderson, and Lonnie Johnson Jr. — all depth/special teams caliber. Safety was critical in Jeff Hafley's Green Bay defense. A to Z Sports identifies this as a 'major need' that 'needs a starter to come from somewhere...most likely the 2026 NFL Draft.'
needs.depth: Lonnie Johnson Jr., Jordan Colbert, Ifeatu Melifonwu — no proven starter

needs.explanation: Traded Jaelan Phillips to Eagles at midseason. Released Bradley Chubb (post-June 1). Lost Matthew Judon to FA. After Chubb's release, Chop Robinson was the only pass rusher with legitimate experience per ESPN. Signed Josh Uche and David Ojabo on one-year prove-it deals, but neither has been consistently productive. Robinson is the only long-term building block. Need a complementary edge rusher with Chop.
needs.depth: David Ojabo, Robert Beal Jr., Cameron Goode — all depth pieces on one-year deals

needs.explanation: Traded Jonnu Smith (2024), Darren Waller likely retired. Re-signed Greg Dulcich (bright spot with 26 rec, 336 yds, 1 TD in 10 games) and added Ben Sims from Green Bay. Dulcich showed promise but the room lacks depth and a true every-down TE. Julian Hill signed with Patriots. New GM Sullivan's offense needs a versatile weapon here.
needs.depth: Ben Sims (TE3 profile per beat writers) — very thin

needs.explanation: Patrick Paul is blossoming into an elite LT. Austin Jackson accepted a pay cut to return at RT but is injury-prone and in a contract year (FA after 2026). Need a developmental RT to eventually replace Jackson. Salyer provides some tackle versatility as a backup. Not an immediate crisis but a clear succession plan needed.
needs.current_starter: LT Patrick Paul (locked in), RT Austin Jackson (injury-prone, contract year)
needs.depth: Jamaree Salyer (swing), Kion Smith — Jackson's injury history makes depth critical

needs.explanation: Jordyn Brooks is the anchor at off-ball LB and is an extension candidate. Re-signed Willie Gay Jr. but he played only 131 defensive snaps in 2025. Tyrel Dodson could be a cap casualty. The room needs youth and athleticism next to Brooks. PFF identifies LB as one of the 'most urgent needs' in the back seven.
needs.depth: Willie Gay Jr. (re-signed), Cameron Goode, K.J. Britt — limited upside

free_agency_impact: Released Tua Tagovailoa (post-June 1 designation) — signed Malik Willis on 3-year/$67.5M deal, QB addressed for now
Released Tyreek Hill (failed physical, ACL) — WR need becomes critical, only Waddle remains as proven receiver
Released Bradley Chubb (post-June 1 designation) — EDGE need elevated, signed Josh Uche and David Ojabo as replacements
Traded Minkah Fitzpatrick to Jets for 7th-round pick — S need becomes critical, no proven starter on roster
Lost CB Kader Kohou to Chiefs — CB need worsens in already-depleted secondary
Lost CB Rasul Douglas (UFA, did not re-sign) — further thins CB room
Lost S Elijah Campbell to Giants — further thins safety room
Lost TE Julian Hill to Patriots (RFA, not tendered) — TE depth hit
Lost FB Alec Ingold to Chargers — followed Mike McDaniel
Released OL James Daniels, OL Liam Eichenberg (failed physical) — IOL need grows
Signed CB Marco Wilson, CB Darrell Baker Jr., CB Alex Austin, CB A.J. Green III (re-signed) — CB depth added but no starter
Signed S Zayne Anderson, S/DB Lonnie Johnson Jr. — safety depth added but no starter
Signed OL Jamaree Salyer — RG competition but likely a backup/swing
Signed WR Tutu Atwell, WR Jalen Tolbert — depth additions, not WR2 solutions
Signed EDGE Josh Uche, EDGE David Ojabo, EDGE Robert Beal Jr. — rotation depth behind Chop Robinson
Re-signed TE Greg Dulcich (1-year/$3.25M), signed TE Ben Sims — TE room partially stabilized
Re-signed LB Willie Gay Jr., DT Matthew Butler — depth retention
Released K Jason Sanders — signed K Zane Gonzalez and re-signed K Riley Patterson to compete
scheme_fit_notes: New HC Jeff Hafley comes from Green Bay where he was DC — expect a defense modeled on the Packers' system, making versatile safeties critical (Hafley's defense leaned heavily on safety play). OC Bobby Slowik brings Shanahan-tree zone-run concepts, elevating the need for athletic offensive linemen who can move laterally. Willis's rushing ability means the offense will incorporate designed QB runs and RPOs, making IOL mobility a priority. CB prospects who can play press-man and zone-match coverage fit best. EDGE rushers with power and run defense ability complement the scheme alongside Chop Robinson's speed.
